positiveClauses.text: A forward migration repairs predecessor membership before the schema version advances.
The migration normalises closed dependency rows for existing records.
The additive persistence change shall backfill the registered scope.
Publication completes before the schema version advances.
Forward repair completes predecessor recovery.
Table replacement preserves committed authority.
Persistence migration preserves committed authority.
Migration preflight upgrades historical state and forward repair completes recovery.
The existing route row gains non-null enforcement fields.
Specs 01 and 04 shall be amended before completion.

negativeClauses.text: An incremental migration number is not an implementation requirement.
No compatibility backfill or forward-repair path exists.
The current baseline rejects any forward migration.
A forward migration is not required.
No schema upgrade populates existing authority rows.
The current baseline rejects a backfill that populates legacy rows.
No compatibility backfill and a forward migration exist.
The fresh baseline installs all current constraints atomically.
The fresh schema baseline populates required authority fields atomically.
Carry-forward repair preserves the accepted current evidence.
The evidence index is additive and the router forwards current notifications.
Schema upgrade and migration are taxonomy labels in this report.
The migration-backfill fixture category names a negative test case.
The current optional feature may advertise a schema-upgrade label.
The pinned compatibility schema rejects unknown versions without retry.
The existing grant row gains a monotone revision after each approved current operation.
`grant_rows` gains a monotone revision after each approved current operation.
The governance register records the current schema version and amendment history.
